fields.description:
# Stage-1 BM25F candidate generation over digests + graph-exclusion filter

## Why
Stage 1 must narrow a few hundred tickets to ~20 candidates cheaply and deterministically, with no LLM and no embeddings, and must NOT surface a ticket's own already-related work (parents/children/siblings/linked) — the top source of "legitimately related but distinct" false flags.

## What
- A brute-force BM25F "More Like This" scorer over the corpus of `ticket_digest` records (from S2). Field-weighted: `problem_keywords` and `component_or_area` high, `propositions`/`key_entities` lower (weights configurable). Query side = the query ticket's freshly-extracted digest (S1), passed in by the caller. Precision knobs (all configurable via the `overlap` typed config): `max_doc_freq` and a stopword list to prune project boilerplate, a `minimum_should_match`-style overlap floor, and `K` (default 20). Returns a typed `OverlapCandidate` list `[{ticket_id, score, matched_terms}]` (this is the interface S7 consumes — stated here so S7 isn't blind to it).
- Graph-exclusion filter applied BEFORE scoring, dropping the query ticket's parents, children, siblings (same parent), and any ticket already linked by any relation. This needs a NEW small shared helper `related_ticket_ids(ticket_id) -> set[str]` (extract the parent-map/BFS logic — `next_batch.py`'s BFS is epic-scoped, private, and NOT reusable as-is; factor the parent-map build into a shared util used by both). Reads go through the reduced ticket state / `ticket_deps` data, not the MCP tool.
- Corpus tickets whose digest is absent or stale (per S2 freshness) are SKIPPED (never matched on raw text — no fallback). "Stale" = S2's content-hash/version mismatch (not an age threshold). Count skipped tickets and log the count so a silently-degraded (cold) run is distinguishable from a healthy one.
- Error handling: a graph-read or scoring exception is caught, logged, and yields an empty candidate list (the overlap step then surfaces nothing) — never propagates to block review_plan.

## Dependencies
S1 (`ticket_digest` schema + fields), S2 (digest store + freshness). Digest fields are DEFINED in S1, not pre-existing code.

## Scope
- New `src/rebar/llm/overlap/retrieve.py` (BM25F + candidate type); new shared `related_ticket_ids` helper (co-located with the extracted parent-map util); `overlap` config weights/knobs in `_config_schema.py`.
- OUT: the LLM judge (S6), review-plan wiring (S7). Interface contract for S7: `retrieve(query_digest, corpus, exclude) -> list[OverlapCandidate]`.

## Testing
- Unit `tests/unit/test_overlap_retrieve.py`: top-K ordering; graph-exclusion drops parents/children/siblings/linked (fixture graph); absent/stale digests skipped + skip-count logged; boilerplate fixture (shared boilerplate, distinct work) does NOT rank as a match; knobs configurable; a graph-read error yields [] not an exception. Perf: assert <10ms for a 300-digest corpus.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Given a query digest and corpus, returns top-K (default 20) `OverlapCandidate`s by field-weighted BM25F; <10ms at ~300 digests; verified by `pytest tests/unit/test_overlap_retrieve.py::{test_topk,test_perf}`.
- [ ] Candidates EXCLUDE the query ticket's parents, children, siblings, and already-linked tickets, via the shared `related_ticket_ids` helper; verified by `::test_graph_exclusion`.
- [ ] Corpus tickets with absent/stale digests (S2 content-hash/version mismatch) are skipped and the skip-count is logged; verified by `::test_cold_skip`.
- [ ] Boilerplate terms do not drive matches (`max_doc_freq`/stopwords); verified by `::test_boilerplate`.
- [ ] Field weights, `max_doc_freq`, overlap floor, and `K` are configurable via the `overlap` typed config; a graph-read/scoring error yields an empty list (never blocks the caller).
